other_observers: John Drummond, Bill Schmoker, Lisa Edwards, Joe Roller, Glenn Walbek, Loch Kilpatrick, Peter Gent, and many others; Glenn Walbek found the bird a couple hours earlier.
specific_location: east of south shore marina at Chatfield Reservoir, Chatfield State Park
description: A large, dark-mantled gull with dull pinkish legs, noticeably larger than nearby Cal gull. Head white without any signs of streaking. Iris light and orbital orange-yellow and contrasting with surrounding white feathers. Bill orangy yellow, bulbous at distal end and wide-based. Noticeable steep angle to gonys with red-orange large spot. Folded wings dark with row of white feathers visible from each shingled primary feather.  Spread wings, dorsal view, P10 with elongated subterminal white spot contrasting with gray on either side. P8 and P9 with small white terminal tip but without white mirrors or subterminal white spots. Mantle slate gray and about two shades darker than adult CAGU which was about 5 feet from the WEGU. Broad white trailing edge to wings in flight. No "string of pearls" in flight. Underwing perched but spread wing (see photo) leading edge white except for outer 6-7 primaries which formed contrasting dark wingtip with elongated white mirror on P-10. Outer 6-7 primaires with small white tips. lighter gray tongue on P6 or P5. Tail white.Behaviors: Mostly loafing, sometimes walking with gimpy leg. Flew twice and landed by dead carp on which it fed. After eacy feeding it walked to the water and drank and splashed water with its head. Sometimes used its left foot to seemingly push at its throat as if something was lodged there. Howerver, swallowed chunks of dead fish with seemingly not trouble.Call: none heardPlumage: adult breeding
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
